stories.headline: UAE to Leave OPEC in May as Iran War Reshapes Oil Market
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates will leave OPEC, dealing a blow to the group and its leader Saudi Arabia as the global oil industry grapples with the massive supply disruption caused by the Iran war. Joumanna Bercetche explains.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UAE Quits OPEC as War Upends Oil Markets and Gulf Tensions Rise
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates will leave OPEC next month, in a significant blow to the group that raises questions about its future at a time when the industry is grappling with the massive supply disruption caused by the Iran war.

stories.headline: UAE leaves OPEC in blow to oil cartel amid war on Iran
stories.summary: The United ‌Arab Emirates has announced it’s withdrawing from OPEC and OPEC+.

stories.headline: UAE leaves OPEC in blow to oil cartel during war on Iran
stories.summary: Announcement comes as Gulf producers are already struggling to ship exports through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: UAE to leave Opec in blow to oil cartel
stories.summary: Move underlines long-running frustrations with group over production quotas

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: Ukraine-Israel row deepens over Russian shipments of stolen grain
stories.summary: In a continuation of the ongoing diplomatic uproar, Zelenskyy publicly criticised Israel on Tuesday for considering a purchase of Ukrainian grain stolen by Russia from temporarily occupied territories in southern Ukraine.

stories.headline: Gulf leaders meet in Saudi Arabia for first time since start of war on Iran
stories.summary: High-level GCC summit in Jeddah embodies 'unified Gulf stance' towards the war, Qatar's emir says.

stories.headline: ‘War crime’: Afghan-Pakistan truce under strain after university strike
stories.summary: Civilian casualties in Kunar spur tensions as Pakistan denies role, casting shadow over ceasefire and peace talks.

stories.headline: Komatsu expects profits to tumble 16% on Iran war, US tariffs
UAE leaves OPEC, citing national interest in 'a new energy age'
stories.summary: The UAE’s historic decision is a major blow to the alliance of oil producers, just as the global energy crisis is escalating over Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, but the Gulf state insists its decision “enhances the UAE’s ability to respond to evolving market needs."

stories.headline: One year after Spain’s blackout, its shift to renewables and grid evolution power on
stories.summary: Though solar was initially incorrectly blamed for crisis, renewables have helped insulate Spain from gas price rises caused by war in Middle EastOne year ago today, all of Spain, and much of Portugal, suffered through a blackout of unprecedented scale and duration. In mere seconds, a cascading sequence of events burst through the grid and created Europe’s first “system black” event in recent memory.Traffic signals failed, mobile networks stopped

stories.headline: Europe’s smaller airports ‘under threat’ if fuel shortages cause many cancellations
stories.summary: High fuel prices and passenger delays as result of EU’s EES entry-exit system leading to problems, says trade bodyBusiness live – latest updatesEurope’s smaller airports may not survive if jet fuel shortages triggered by the Middle East crisis lead to widespread route cancellations, the industry’s trade body has warned.Although airlines insist there are currently no supply problems within the normal four- to six-week horizon, the US-Israel war on

stories.headline: Belarus eyes Western ties as it frees journalist Andrzej Poczobut
stories.summary: President Alexandr Lukashenko is hoping to improve relations with the West once more.

stories.headline: Amnesty calls for US strike on Yemen to be investigated as war crime
stories.summary: The attack last year on a migrant detention facility killed at least 68 people.

stories.headline: Bangladesh nears nuclear debut as energy crisis deepens
South Korean lawmakers push back on US Coupang claims
Lebanon families face second displacement amid ongoing warnings
stories.summary: Families displaced from southern Lebanon set up tents again in Beirut after renewed Israeli warnings.

stories.headline: Qatar warns Iran it will not be used as 'political punching bag'
stories.summary: Qatar warned Iran on Tuesday it will not be used as a “political punching bag,” calling for a "comprehensive deal" that would secure the Gulf. In response to Euronews, Qatar's foreign ministry spokesman said "we applaud our partners in Europe for taking the lead" in the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

stories.headline: Iran war will trigger largest energy price surge since 2022, World Bank warns
stories.summary: The World Bank’s latest Commodity Markets Outlook report predicts a 24% surge in energy prices this year as the Iran war delivers a historic shock to global supply chains.

stories.headline: How the Iran war boosted profits at BP and Barclays
stories.summary: Barclays' profits rose, but loan losses weighed on its first-quarter results. At the same time, oil giant BP’s earnings jumped, largely driven by an exceptional oil trading boom linked to the Iran war.

stories.headline: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
stories.summary: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

stories.headline: Ukraine accuses Israel of receiving shipments of grain 'stolen' by Russia
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says it has received no evidence to support the claim that a vessel carrying grain from occupied Ukraine has reached Haifa.

stories.headline: NZ axes plan for WW2 sex slaves statue after Japan protest
stories.summary: Tokyo had warned that installing the structure in Auckland could jeopardise diplomatic relations.

stories.headline: Argentina Hunts for LNG Imports for Winter in War-Wrecked Market
stories.summary: Argentina is looking to purchase liquefied natural gas cargoes for its upcoming winter heating season, adding demand to a market already squeezed by the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Sherwin-Williams Sees Input Costs Rising After Sales Beat
stories.summary: Sherwin-Williams Co. warned rising input costs could weigh on results later this year, after currency tailwinds helped it post stronger-than-expected sales growth in the first quarter.

stories.headline: Barclays Eyes Over 50% Recovery on MFS as Asset Hunt Uncertain
stories.summary: Barclays Plc hopes to get back much of the £500 million ($674 million) it lent to Market Financial Solutions Ltd. even as insolvency officials overseeing the now-collapsed mortgage lender remain uncertain on how much can be recovered.

stories.headline: Trump Tests the Limits of His Most Faithful Supporters
stories.summary: The president’s rhetoric on religion, along with hard-line immigration policies and the U.S. war in Iran, has splintered a coalition of Christian voters who returned him to the White House.

stories.headline: King Charles Will Speak of ‘Reconciliation and Renewal’ During Address to Congress
stories.summary: The state visit of King Charles III comes at a moment of tension over the war in Iran between President Trump and Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

stories.headline: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in past week
stories.summary: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted elsewhere in regionThree vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry.The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tan

stories.headline: Journalist Andrzej Poczobut freed as part of US-brokered Polish-Belarusian prisoner swap – as it happened
stories.summary: Sakharov prize winner was sentenced to eight years in a penal colony in Belarus in 2021 Polish-Belarusian journalist Andrzej Poczobut, the 2025 Sakharov prize winner, has been freed from Belarusian prison.His release has been confirmed by Poland’s prime minister Donald Tusk, who posted a picture of him on social media saying: “Andrzej Poczobut is free! Welcome to your Polish home, my friend.”“Both have paid a heavy price for speaking truth to pow

stories.headline: Journalist Andrzej Poczobut freed from prison in Belarus in US-brokered swap deal
stories.summary: Sakharov prize winner was given eight-year sentence after process widely condemned as politically motivatedThe Polish-Belarusian journalist Andrzej Poczobut, the 2025 Sakharov prize winner, has been freed after five years in a Belarusian penal colony as part of a US-brokered multi-country swap deal.His release has been confirmed by Poland’s prime minister, Donald Tusk, who posted a picture of him on social media, saying: “Andrzej Poczobut is free

stories.headline: US has 'no closer friends than the British,' Trump tells King Charles III at White House ceremony
stories.summary: Trump’s up-and-down relationship with UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has taken a particularly sour turn over the past several months as the president has sought to rally international support for the Iran war.

stories.headline: US political violence generates a familiar cycle - this time it's in overdrive
stories.summary: In modern America, it seems violence of this kind has become an ever-present storm that can strike anywhere and at any moment.

stories.headline: Japan Airlines trials humanoid robots as ground handlers
stories.summary: These robots may in future help clean cabins and operate ground support equipment.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Oil price climbs above $110 for first time in three weeks
stories.summary: Brent crude rises after White House reiterates ‘red lines’ in negotiations with Tehran

stories.headline: RAC pumps the brakes on £5bn London IPO
stories.summary: Driving group flotation will probably take place in late 2026 as volatile markets push timelines

stories.headline: Emerging Assets Drop as Risk-Off Mood Lifts Dollar Ahead of Fed
stories.summary: Emerging-market currencies and stocks dropped as concerns over investments in artificial intelligence and stalled peace talks in the Middle East sparked a flight to safety in global markets.

stories.headline: Mamdani Pushes to Shrink NY Hedge-Fund, Private-Equity Tax Break
stories.summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is delaying the city’s $127 billion budget as he pressures the state to limit a tax credit used by hedge funds, private equity firms and other businesses.

stories.headline: Netherlands Rejects Argentina Soy Meal Cargoes on GMO Issue
stories.summary: The Netherlands has rejected at least two Argentinian soybean meal cargoes this month after non-approved genetically modified material was found, threatening to disrupt a major trade flow.

stories.headline: Airtel Eyes $2 Billion London IPO of Mobile Money Unit
stories.summary: Airtel Africa Plc’s initial public offering of its mobile money business could raise between $1.5 billion and $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: The king’s visit comes amid foreign policy disagreements with the United States.
Adopting Trump’s Voice, Justice Dept. Asks Judge to Let Ballroom Proceed
stories.summary: The court filing links the security breach at the White House correspondents’ dinner to the lawsuit over President Trump’s ballroom project.

stories.headline: Texas Lawmakers Question Camp Mystic’s Owners Over Deadly Flood
stories.summary: A state senator told the owners of the camp, where 28 people died, that they would no longer be allowed to operate the camp “if I can have anything to say with that.”

stories.headline: Northern Beaches hospital handed to NSW government, ending troubled public-private partnership
stories.summary: Transition and legal handover of hospital from private operator Healthscope to NSW Health occurs at 7am on WednesdayFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastSydney’s Northern Beaches hospital is officially entering the public system, ending a troubled eight-year public-private partnership, although uncertainty about the future of private services remains.The transition and le

stories.headline: Maldives police raid news outlet over report alleging president’s affair
stories.summary: Police seize Adhadhu's computers over documentary alleging an affair between President Mohamed Muizzu and a former aide.

stories.headline: Australia’s top diplomat visits China to talk energy security

What China holds in store for Apple's new CEO
Measures needed to expand 'outside-China' rare earths industry: Lynas CEO
BYD Q1 profit down 55% from year ago as it banks on overseas sales
Japan vows to deepen defense ties with Australia at Nikkei forum in Perth
Southeast Asia's deep-tech startups look to Japan for growth
India drops 9m voters, or 10% of total, weeks before West Bengal poll
Mongolia courts Kazakhstan, seeks path between China and Russia
European Parliament demands €200 billion extra for EU budget in hardball talks
stories.summary: The European Parliament has adopted its position on the EU long-term budget, calling for a 10% increase and pushing for EU-level taxes. MEPs say they are ready for tough talks as money talks intensify for 2028-2034 cycle.

stories.headline: Thousands filled Amsterdam’s streets and canals to celebrate the king’s birthday
stories.summary: Orange crowds fill the streets of Amsterdam as part of the Dutch monarch Willem-Alexander's birthday celebrations.

stories.headline: German bid to rescue 'Timmy' the whale stranded off Baltic coast passes key hurdle
stories.summary: The whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on 23 March before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.

stories.headline: Finland becomes first in Europe to run full lithium mine-to-refinery cycle
stories.summary: Europe’s first complete lithium mine begins operating in Syväjärvi in western Finland. The strategic resource is critical for manufacturing modern electronics.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
stories.summary: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

stories.headline: US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185
stories.summary: Military video shows boat moving swiftly in water before explosion leaves it in flamesThe US military said on Sunday three men were killed when it struck a boat it claimed was “engaged in narco-trafficking operations” in the Eastern Pacific Ocean.This latest strike – which follows dozens of similar attacks on alleged drug boats in recent months – brings the US campaign’s death toll to at least 185, according to a tally compiled by Agence France-P

stories.headline: Investigators in Trump Assassination Attempt Look Through Bluesky for Suspect’s Motive
stories.summary: Federal authorities are looking into whether Cole Tomas Allen posted on Bluesky as “coldforce,” who wrote and promoted liberal views that did not stand out on the left-leaning platform.

stories.headline: Canada's Carney launches a sovereign wealth fund. What is it?
stories.summary: The fund - which Canadians can invest in directly - will help pay for major infrastructure projects in the country, the prime minister says.

stories.headline: Stocks Fall as Report on OpenAI Fuels Tech Jitters: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A selloff in technology giants dragged down stocks as concerns resurfaced over whether vast investments in artificial intelligence will pay off just as traders gear up for a slew of megacap results.

stories.headline: Number of executions in North Korea rose dramatically during Covid – report
stories.summary: Regime used its isolation after closing borders to escalate killings when global scrutiny disappeared, NGO claimsNorth Korea dramatically increased its use of the death penalty after closing its borders during the Covid-19 pandemic, using its isolation to escalate killings when international scrutiny disappeared, according to a report mapping 13 years of executions under the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un.The number of documented cases of executio

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.summary: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.headline: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
stories.summary: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfiresAs wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods.As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition: anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s

stories.headline: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
stories.summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

stories.headline: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.summary: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: Shooting sparks safety fears ahead of World Cup
stories.summary: The Mexican government says the country is safe to visit for the World Cup.

stories.headline: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire to be extended by three weeks

stories.headline: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.summary: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.headline: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.
